prompt:
Ticket: replace our crusty bash+sed blog build with a real generator. Plain TypeScript on node, zero packages — we pipe the output Map into our uploader ourselves, so no filesystem work needed. The acceptance tests import three modules; anything else you want to factor out is your call. posts.ts — front-matter parsing: - `parsePost(source)` -> { title, date, tags, slug, draft, body }. A post starts with a `---` line, then `key: value` lines (split on the FIRST colon, so 'title: Re: the launch' works; blank lines inside the block are ignored), then a closing `---`; everything after is the body with leading/trailing whitespace stripped (a `---` later in the body is just body). No opening fence or no closing fence -> Error mentioning 'front matter'. - keys: title (required, non-empty), date (required, YYYY-MM-DD and a REAL calendar date — 2024-02-30 must be rejected, 2024-02-29 accepted; the error names the bad value), tags (optional, comma-separated), draft (optional, 'true'/'false' only, default false), slug (optional, used verbatim when given). Any other key -> Error naming it. - `slugify(s)`: lowercase, every run of non [a-z0-9] becomes one '-', trim leading/trailing '-'. Default slug = slugify(title). Tags are each run through slugify too, empties dropped, dupes dropped keeping first-seen order. render.ts — the markdown-ish body renderer: - `escapeHtml(s)` escaping & < > " ; `renderMarkdown(body)` -> HTML string, blocks joined by '\n', empty/whitespace body -> ''. - blocks are separated by one-or-more blank lines. '# ', '## ', '### ' start h1/h2/h3 (four #'s or no space = just a paragraph). A block where lines start '- ' is `
    ` with one `
  • ` per line (newlines between li's, like
      \n
    • a
      • \n
    • b
      • \n
    ). Anything else is a `

    `, its internal newlines collapsed to single spaces. - inline, applied inside headings, list items and paragraphs: `code` spans first (contents escaped but NEVER treated as markup — `**argv**` inside backticks stays literal), then escaping, [text](url) links, **strong**, *em*. Ampersands in a URL come out as & in the href. site.ts — `buildSite(sources: string[])` -> Map: - posts/.html for every non-draft post:

    \n

    {title}

    \n

    {date}

    \n{rendered body}\n
    and when the post has tags the meta line is instead: {date} · tags: {links}, links '{tag}' joined by ', ', tags sorted alphabetically. - index.html: '

    Archive

    ' then
      of '
    • {title} — {date}
      • ', newest date first, ties by slug ascending. - tags/{tag}.html per tag: '

      Tag: {tag}

      ' + the same archive-style list but hrefs '../posts/{slug}.html', newest first. - tags/index.html: '

      Tags

      ' +
        of '
      • {tag} ({count})
        • ' alphabetically. - Titles are escaped everywhere they appear. Drafts produce no page and count nowhere (a tag used only by drafts has no page and no index entry). Two posts landing on the same slug -> Error naming the slug. Any parse error aborts the build. Empty input still yields index.html and tags/index.html with empty lists. Acceptance: `node --test test_posts.ts test_render.ts test_site.ts` — those three files are the contract, leave them alone.
